stories.body: U.S. forces destroyed Iranian surveillance radar positions at Goruk and Qeshm Island after shooting down four Iranian drones that Central Command said were aimed at regional maritime traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, adding to tensions as Washington and Tehran struggle to reach an interim deal to halt their three-month-old war; President Donald Trump said Iran retains only about 22% of its missile arsenal, while Tehran insists any ceasefire must include Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon and full leverage over the key oil chokepoint.
stories.headline: U.S. strikes Iranian coastal radar sites after drone attacks on shipping

stories.body: Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi told Lebanon’s Al Mayadeen that Washington must secure an Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon and a ceasefire with Hezbollah before Tehran will agree to pause its broader conflict with the United States, highlighting how Hezbollah attacks on Israeli forces and U.S. naval interventions against Iranian shipping have become entwined with negotiations over sanctions relief, access to oil revenues, and control of the Strait of Hormuz amid surging regional violence and economic pressure.
stories.headline: Iran ties any U.S. peace deal to Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon

stories.body: Kyiv officials reported fires at a Podil district industrial site, a nine-story apartment block and multiple car lots after debris from fresh Russian missile barrages hit the Ukrainian capital, prompting Mayor Vitali Klitschko to urge residents into shelters while President Volodymyr Zelenskiy warned that intelligence still points to a possible massive strike as Moscow vows “systematic” attacks on military targets in Kyiv in retaliation for alleged Ukrainian drone operations in occupied Luhansk.
stories.headline: Kyiv suffers heavy Russian strikes amid renewed missile threats

stories.body: NASA directed five astronauts into their Crew Dragon lifeboat for about two hours after an air leak aboard Russia’s Zvezda module doubled to two pounds per day, but lifted the evacuation order once Roscosmos paused its plan to access the crack with a saw, with both agencies now seeking a joint repair approach even as the station’s seven-person crew continues scientific operations and lawmakers push legislation extending ISS operations to 2032.
stories.headline: NASA reverses ISS evacuation order after sealing air leak

stories.body: An Austrian court opened a monthlong trial of Khaled al-H, the former head of Syria’s General Intelligence Directorate in Raqqa, and a senior police aide known as the “Angel of Death,” on charges including torture, sexual assault and aggravated coercion stemming from the Assad regime’s crackdown between 2011 and 2013; both defendants pleaded not guilty, denying knowledge of alleged overcrowded cells, beatings with hoses and the “magic carpet” torture device, while prosecutors plan to present victim testimony under Austria’s universal jurisdiction.
stories.headline: Austria tries former Syrian intelligence chief for torture and sexual abuse

stories.body: Healthcare magnate Rick Jackson leveraged more than $100 million of personal spending to overwhelm endorsements from President Donald Trump and Governor Brian Kemp and win Georgia’s Republican gubernatorial runoff, reshaping the state’s GOP landscape and setting up a November matchup with Democrat Keisha Lance Bottoms as Republicans debate how Jackson’s late conversion to MAGA politics and record-breaking ad barrage will influence down-ballot races.
stories.headline: Billionaire Rick Jackson defeats Trump-backed Burt Jones in Georgia governor runoff

stories.body: After a bruising primary, Rep. Mike Collins secured the GOP nomination to challenge Sen. Jon Ossoff, but Republican strategists fret over Collins’ limited cash, hardline positions, ethics scrutiny and the Democrat’s $32 million war chest, warning that national committees and billionaire gubernatorial nominee Rick Jackson must quickly inject resources if the party hopes to flip Georgia’s marquee Senate seat in November.
stories.headline: Republicans still worried about beating Sen. Jon Ossoff despite runoff victories

stories.body: In a 52-47 pre-dawn vote, Senate Republicans advanced a bill adding $70 billion for ICE and Border Patrol operations while blocking Democratic efforts to permanently outlaw a $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund favored by President Trump, despite internal GOP defections and failed amendments from Senators Susan Collins, Jon Husted, Dan Sullivan and Thom Tillis who warned the fund—already frozen by DOJ—poses political risks ahead of the midterms.
stories.headline: U.S. Senate approves $70 billion ICE funding but refuses to kill Trump ‘anti-weaponization’ fund

stories.body: As Washington, D.C., heads toward primaries that will replace 12-year Mayor Muriel Bowser and long-serving Delegate Eleanor Holmes Norton, frontrunners Janeese Lewis George, Kenyan McDuffie, Robert White and Brooke Pinto are campaigning on resistance to President Trump’s federalization of local policing, National Guard deployments and immigration crackdowns, signaling likely clashes over home rule, economic recovery projects and the administration’s efforts to reshape the capital.
stories.headline: Washington braces for new leaders likely to confront Trump over policing and autonomy

stories.body: Former Prime Minister José Luis Rodríguez Zapatero is set to become the first ex-leader of Spain’s democracy to appear as an investigated party before the Audiencia Nacional, where Judge José Luis Calama will question him about alleged irregularities in the Plus Ultra airline rescue and undeclared jewelry worth more than €1 million, a hearing that coincides with a heated parliamentary control session and mounting pressure on Pedro Sánchez’s government.
stories.headline: Zapatero to testify before judge over Plus Ultra bailout and €1.3 million in undeclared jewelry

stories.body: Facing a Senate Internal Affairs committee dominated by the PP, Civil Guard director Mercedes González repeatedly insisted she never interfered with UCO corruption probes that implicate the PSOE or coordinated with former party member Leire Díez, saying she has not met with UCO agents about active cases or visited their facilities since taking office in September 2024 despite opposition claims of a plot to shield the government.
stories.headline: Civil Guard director Mercedes González denies trying to derail UCO investigations or taking cues from Leire Díez

stories.body: Using their majority on the Congreso’s governing Mesa, PSOE and Sumar refused to admit amendments from Junts and the PP that would have forced a symbolic vote urging Pedro Sánchez to dissolve parliament, arguing that calling elections is an exclusive constitutional power of the prime minister; conservatives denounced the veto as proof the coalition fears losing a test of confidence.
stories.headline: Congress leadership blocks PP and Junts bids to demand early general elections

stories.body: During a preliminary hearing in Judge Juan Carlos Peinado’s case against Begoña Gómez over the Plus Ultra rescue, the far-right group Hazte Oír, acting as popular prosecution, requested that the prime minister’s wife surrender her passport, receive a travel ban and report biweekly to court while it pursues a 24-year sentence, though the judge has yet to rule on the proposed precautionary measures.
stories.headline: Hazte Oír-led accusation asks judge to seize Begoña Gómez’s passport and restrict travel

stories.body: Barcelona’s PSC-ERC-Comuns alliance agreed to raise the municipal surcharge on cruise passengers who stay less than 12 hours from €8 to €24 via a Catalan budget amendment, which combined with the regional levy will bring the total tax to €30 as Deputy Mayor Jordi Valls argues that fiscal tools must discourage high-impact day-trippers and fund local services even as details on timing and phasing remain to be negotiated.
stories.headline: Barcelona coalition backs €30 tax on short-stop cruise passengers to curb day-tripper pressure

stories.body: After a six-hour meeting, the committee representing Consorci de Biblioteques staff said it will ask the Catalan Labor Department to mediate because the city refused to scrap 11-hour workdays or drop a proposed absenteeism plan for weekend employees, despite offering 25 new hires, leaving strikes that began May 26 unresolved and unions accusing city hall of ignoring a May 29 council mandate.
stories.headline: Talks on Barcelona library strike collapse again as unions seek mediation over 11-hour shifts

stories.body: Barcelona’s urban planning commission granted final approval to Núñez y Navarro’s plan to convert the upper floors of Domènech i Montaner’s Casa Lleó Morera from mostly vacant offices into museum space, resolving earlier concerns about mobility, environmental and accessibility compliance so the last closed landmark on the “block of discord” can eventually receive visitors without altering protected facades or displacing the Loewe store at street level.
stories.headline: City clears path for Casa Lleó Morera to reopen to the public on Passeig de Gràcia

stories.body: With heat waves worsening, Barcelona activated over 500 climate refuges through September 15—100 more than last year—by adding libraries, public facilities and about 60 volunteer businesses so that 99% of residents have a cool space within a 10-minute walk even on Sundays in August, while the city also extends water play areas, shade projects like the Moll de la Fusta canopy and special programming in five libraries to mitigate health impacts on vulnerable people.
stories.headline: Barcelona to expand summer network of heat refuges to more than 500 sites, adding private venues
